author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
content:
RHEUMATOLOGY URGENT FOLLOW-UP — 11/9/2026, 9:00 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Visit source: Urgent re-evaluation 5 days after Hopkins ED visit 11/4/2026.
Funded via Hopkins financial-aid grant (1 of 2 covered visits).
Interpreter: Spanish (Language Line).

INTERVAL EVENTS:
As documented in ED note 11/4/2026 (Dr. Shah): Patient presented with worsening polyarthritis, fatigue, weight loss to 43.8 kg (13.8% loss), photosensitive rash, mild cytopenias (WBC 3.10, Hgb 10.2, Plt 184), low albumin (3.0), newly-positive anti-dsDNA (24), low complements (C3 76, C4 8), and trace proteinuria (UPC 0.35). Patient declined admission. Started on prednisone 30 mg PO daily + restarted hydroxychloroquine 200 mg BID via charity bridge. Sent home with strong follow-up plan.

Since 11/4/2026 (5 days):
- Taking prednisone 30 mg daily and HCQ 200 mg BID consistently.
- Reports modest improvement: joint pain is somewhat better; she could grip her toothbrush this morning, which she could not do last week. Morning stiffness now ~60 minutes.
- Fatigue is unchanged — still profound. She has not returned to work.
- No new symptoms. Specifically denies: fever, chest pain, dyspnea, oral ulcers, abdominal pain, dark/foamy urine, leg swelling, dysphagia, focal weakness, vision changes.
- Appetite slightly improved on prednisone but she remains hesitant about food due to food insecurity. Mother reports cooking what she can.
- Mood: PHQ-9 today 12 (still moderate). She accepted referral to Esperanza Behavioral Health; intake call scheduled for next week.

VITALS: BP 102/64, HR 92, RR 14, T 98.0 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 44.4 kg (up 0.6 kg from ED 11/4/2026). Height 150 cm. BMI 19.7.

EXAM:
General: Thin young woman, mildly cachectic. Appears slightly less ill than ED report describes; cooperative.
Skin: Faint blanching pink rash on chest V-area and upper back, less prominent than ED documented. No new lesions. No oral ulcers. Diffuse scalp hair thinning unchanged.
MSK: Improved synovitis at MCPs and wrists — still tender but less swollen than 9/15/2026 exam. Left knee effusion smaller. Proximal muscle strength: 4+/5 hip flexion, 5/5 shoulder abduction. Grip 4/5 bilaterally (improved from 3/5 on 11/4).
Neuro: Grossly intact.
Lymph nodes: No adenopathy. No oral ulcers.

LABS (drawn today):
- CBC: WBC 3.40 (low), Hgb 10.5 (low), Plt 198 (low-normal); ANC 1.8; lymphocytes 1.0
- CMP: Cr 0.5, BUN 8, Albumin 3.1 (still low), Total protein 6.2
- ESR 38 (improved from 52), CRP 1.4 (improved from 2.3)
- Anti-dsDNA 22 (unchanged from 24)
- C3 80 (slightly up from 76), C4 9 (still low)
- UPC 0.28 (improved from 0.35)
- Glucose 108 (slightly elevated — likely steroid)
- TSH 4.78 (mild elevation persists)

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

23-year-old woman with newly-classified active SLE (cumulative criteria: ANA, immunologic with low complement + dsDNA, hematologic with leukopenia/lymphopenia/mild anemia/borderline thrombocytopenia, mucocutaneous with photosensitive rash, constitutional with weight loss, possibly renal with mild proteinuria), 5 days into intensified therapy with prednisone 30 mg daily and HCQ 200 mg BID.

Clinical improvement is evident — joint exam, grip strength, energy slightly better. Lab markers slightly improved (ESR down, CRP down, UPC down). However, anti-dsDNA and complements have not yet normalized, weight has only minimally recovered, and she remains highly underweight.

I am intensifying immunosuppression today by adding a steroid-sparing agent. After discussion of options (mycophenolate, azathioprine, methotrexate) and review of her childbearing potential and adherence challenges, we are starting mycophenolate mofetil. I have given an explicit, in-depth discussion of teratogenicity (with mother and patient through interpreter), the need for contraception, and the alternative of switching to a non-teratogenic agent (azathioprine) if pregnancy becomes desired. Patient currently not sexually active and not planning conception in next year; understands and accepts.

PLAN:
1. Start mycophenolate mofetil 500 mg PO BID for 2 weeks, then increase to 1 g BID if tolerated. Counseled on GI side effects, infection risk, contraception requirement.
2. Continue prednisone 30 mg PO daily for 2 more weeks; then begin slow taper (decrease by 5 mg every 2 weeks). Counseled on glucose, infection signs, mood changes, sleep.
3. Continue hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID. Need baseline ophthalmology — Hopkins ophtho coordinator engaged for charity slot.
4. Continue levothyroxine.
5. Continue calcium 500 mg BID and vitamin D 1000 IU daily.
6. Glucose monitoring at home (provided meter through clinic).
7. PPI continued (omeprazole 20 mg daily) for prophylaxis on prednisone + MMF.
8. Pre-immunosuppression infection screen: TB-IGRA (drawn today), hepatitis B/C (negative per January labs but repeating to confirm at current state), HIV (repeat). Send out today.
9. Vaccinations: Reviewed — patient overdue for influenza, Tdap, HPV. Will refer to Esperanza for non-live vaccines. NO live vaccines on immunosuppression.
10. Mycophenolate PAP application submitted today by Dr. Tanaka's office. Should provide 6-month supply if approved (~70% approval rate based on her status).
11. Outpatient labs: CBC, CMP, ESR, CRP, UPC, anti-dsDNA, complements — repeat 11/30/2026.
12. Follow-up: 3 weeks with me (target 11/30/2026) if MMF tolerated; sooner if any concerns.
13. Return precautions reviewed in Spanish; same as ED.

Visit time: 70 minutes (interpreter 38 min).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Jessica Tanaka, MD
Rheumatology, Johns Hopkins
Date: 11/9/2026
